header.description: Report metadata scalars rendered in the header and footer. report_date is a user-supplied display parameter. avg_ph and exceedance_count are post-query aggregates computed at pipeline level; they are UNRESOLVED in the declarative contract and must be injected by the pipeline after row data is fetched.

rows.description: One row per timestamp from neuract__ANALYSER_TABLE within the optional date_from/date_to window. Each row carries the formatted timestamp and the four simultaneous pH channel readings.

totals.description: No totals rows are defined in this report template.

semantics: date_from and date_to are optional filters applied to neuract__ANALYSER_TABLE.timestamp_utc to bound the returned row set. report_date is a pass-through parameter rendered directly in the HTML header. ph_threshold is an optional pass-through used exclusively by pipeline code to compute exceedance_count; it does not appear as a template token and is not applied as a row filter.
transformations: row_timestamp is formatted from UTC timestamp to display string using format '%d-%m-%Y %H:%M:%S' via format_date row_computed op.
row_ph_101 through row_ph_104 are displayed with 4-decimal precision using number(4) formatter, appropriate for pH sensor engineering standards.

avg_ph is the scalar mean of all PH_101, PH_102, PH_103, and PH_104 values across the entire filtered row set; pipeline must compute this as df[['PH_101','PH_102','PH_103','PH_104']].values.mean() (UNRESOLVED declaratively).

exceedance_count is the total count of individual pH channel readings across all four channels and all filtered rows where the value exceeds the ph_threshold parameter; pipeline must compute this with a boolean mask and sum (UNRESOLVED declaratively).
Rows are ordered ascending by neuract__ANALYSER_TABLE.timestamp_utc.
